summary: Large fighter-collision migration that removed most of asm/melee/ft/ftcoll.s and moved the logic into src/melee/ft/ftcoll.c, while tightening related fighter collision structs, bitfield accessors, and function prototypes. The PR body describes it as mostly C migrations with some matches by coauthors; diff evidence shows both C cleanup and remaining MWERKS inline asm blocks for some fcoll routines, so it should be treated as a major source migration rather than a fully semantic rename pass.
what_changed: Deleted 2754 lines from asm/melee/ft/ftcoll.s and added roughly 2987 lines to src/melee/ft/ftcoll.c.

Added or migrated fcoll routines around combo-count handling, victim/attacker tracking, hitbox/hurtbox collision, shield/collision checks, and fighter interaction state; visible examples include func_800763C0, func_80076444, func_8007646C, func_800764DC, func_80076528, func_800765AC, func_800765E0, and func_800765F0.

Preserved some routines as MWERKS inline asm inside fcoll.c with typed asm signatures, such as func_8007699C(Fighter*, Hitbox*, Fighter*, Hitbox*), func_80076CBC(Fighter*, Hitbox*, Fighter*), and func_80076ED8(Fighter*, Hitbox*, Fighter*, Hurtbox*).
Changed fcoll prototypes from untyped void-style declarations to typed boolean-returning and argument-specific declarations in fcoll.h.

Expanded Fighter bitfield representation around offsets 0x221B and 0x2225, replacing generic x221B_flag.bits and x2225_flag.bits usage in multiple files with direct fields such as x221B_b0 through x221B_b7 and x2225_b0 through x2225_b7.

Adjusted many character/fighter call sites to use the new direct bitfield names, including CLink, Mars counter code, Master Hand, Purin, Yoshi, fighter initialization/action-change code, and ftcommon helpers.

Refined lbcollision.h's Hitbox layout by splitting padding to expose sfx_severity at 0x38, sfx_kind at 0x3C, x42/x43 bitfields, and a union at 0x134 containing either HSD_GObj* owner or hit_grabbed_victim_only.
Updated thrown-hitbox clearing from fp->x1064_thrownHitbox.x134 = 0 to fp->x1064_thrownHitbox.owner = NULL, reflecting the improved Hitbox union typing.
Added missing prototypes in ft_unknown_006.h, gm/code_801601C4.h, lbcollision.h, pl_unknown_001.h, and ftlib.h to support the migrated fcoll code.
Renamed at least one Fighter field from x119D to grabber_unk1, suggesting the offset's use was inferred from grab/collision handling.
smart_moves: Kept the migration buildable by improving headers and prototypes alongside the fcoll source movement, instead of leaving migrated code dependent on local extern declarations only.
Used direct bitfield members for hot collision flags at 0x221B and 0x2225, which reduced repeated generic UnkFlagStruct access and made matching against lbz/rlwinm flag patterns more explicit.
Improved Hitbox layout incrementally where fcoll needed real semantics: sfx fields, x42/x43 flags, and the 0x134 owner/grab union were exposed without pretending the full struct was solved.
Retained an explicit warning in lbcollision.h that the Hitbox 0x134 union is unacceptable, which documents a known compromise instead of hiding uncertain layout semantics.
Used typed callback aliases such as HSD_GObjEvent for func_8007B1B8 rather than a raw void (*)(HSD_GObj*) signature.
Converted helper prototypes to bool where call sites test return values, e.g. func_8007699C, func_80076CBC, func_80076ED8, and lbcollision helpers.
Moved item-owner combo logic to clearer names in func_8007646C, using owner and enum_t asid and checking func_80086960(owner) before forwarding combo credit.
decomp_lessons: Large assembly migrations often require struct/header work first; fcoll needed Fighter, Hitbox, Hurtbox, ftlib, gm, and pl prototypes to express the code cleanly.
When a generic flag byte blocks matching or readability, expose direct bitfields at the exact byte offset and update call sites consistently.
Do not over-resolve uncertain unions: if one field is used as a pointer in one context and a bit in another, a documented union can be an acceptable interim decomp tactic.
Typed boolean returns are useful when migrated collision routines are immediately used in conditionals; this avoids void stubs that obscure control flow.
For migrated code that still has nonmatching or hard-to-express sections, moving asm into C under MWERKS pragmas can reduce standalone .s debt while preserving exact behavior.
Comments like @todo #GET_FIGHTER adds an instruction are valuable matching notes; they record why apparently idiomatic macros may not be used in a matching function.
Header prototype corrections in dependent systems can be a prerequisite for source migration even when the primary PR target is one fighter subsystem.
naming_conventions: Direct unknown bitfields were named with offset-plus-bit form, e.g. x221B_b0, x221B_b5, x2225_b2, preserving address evidence while improving access syntax.
Generic flag structs remain named xNNNN_flag where their bit layout has not been broken out or required for matching.
Improved inferred semantic names are conservative: x119D became grabber_unk1 rather than a fully speculative name.
Hitbox fields exposed from padding use semantic names when evidence is stronger, such as sfx_severity, sfx_kind, owner, and hit_grabbed_victim_only.
Function names remain address-based func_800..., but comments describe roles such as Combo Count Logic, Get Attack ID, Item Owner, and Check to end combo for victim.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed most standalone fcoll.s assembly while keeping some routines as inline asm in fcoll.c under MWERKS_GEKKO pragmas, preserving exact codegen where C was not yet matched.
Used typed asm function signatures to make inline asm blocks callable from C with correct argument and return types.
Avoided GET_FIGHTER in at least one place because the diff comment says it adds an instruction; direct gobj->user_data access was used for matching.
Introduced inline ftHitGetPtr(Fighter* fp, u32 i) returning &fp->x914[i] to centralize hitbox indexing in the migrated collision code.
Changed padding and bitfield definitions in lbcollision.h to make lbz/rlwinm accesses map to named fields at offsets 0x40, 0x42, 0x43, and 0x134.
Used bool prototypes for collision-check helper functions so branches testing collision results line up naturally in C.
Preserved exact uncertain behavior by keeping a union for Hitbox offset 0x134 rather than forcing one field interpretation across all call sites.

review_feedback.note: No issue comments or review comments were present in the slice, so there is no detailed human review feedback to extract.

searchable_terms: Migrate most of ftcoll to C
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace the remaining inline asm functions in src/melee/ft/ftcoll.c with matching C.
Search for subsequent cleanup of the lbcollision.h Hitbox 0x134 union marked '@todo This union is unacceptable.'
Track later renames for x221B_b* and x2225_b* to learn their actual fighter collision semantics.
Search for follow-up struct work around Fighter offset x119D / grabber_unk1.
Inspect later PRs touching func_8007699C, func_80076CBC, and func_80076ED8 for final prototypes and matching tactics.
